items.title: High-Quality Streaming Apps on Amazon Fire TV: Introducing the Blueprint
items.description: When viewers open a streaming app on Amazon Fire TV, they expect it to start quickly, be easy to navigate with a remote, and begin playback reliably. items.title: Evenly schedule pods using Topology Spread Constraints in AWS EKS
items.description:
The Kubernetes Topology Spread Constraints allow scheduling pods evenly across nodes, regions and availability zones.

The following TopologySpreadConstraints ensure that at any given time, the difference in the number of pods between two nodes never exceeds 2.

items.title: A HAR file is an auth dump: a 30-second safe-sharing checklist
items.description:
When support asks for a HAR file, the request sounds harmless: reproduce the bug, export a log, attach it to a ticket.

But a HAR is not just a performance trace. It can be a snapshot of an authenticated browser session.

items.description:

items.title: The Person Who Fixed the Bugs Just Vanished
items.description:
We've been testing a new project this week.

The project's origin story is a mess. Upper management used AI to generate an HTML demo, then handed it to a team called "Super Individual Department" — a pure-AI coding unit they'd set up a few months ago. No product manager, no requirements doc

items.title: I Was Optimizing Ranking While the Real Problem Was Selection
items.description:
I mistook movement for improvement.

For three months, I changed our ranking algorithm every two weeks.

  • BM25.
  • Hybrid BM25 + vector search.
  • A cross-encoder reranker.
  • A different embedding model.
  • Yet another embedding model.
